expectations.text: Argues the claim is overstated or a measurement artifact
expectations.evidence: States: 'triceps do not become the prime mover near max loads' and calls it 'a measurement artifact.'

expectations.text: Explains the long head triceps biarticular mechanism
expectations.evidence: INCORRECTLY states pecs are biarticular and triceps are monoarticular. The expected answer notes the long head triceps is biarticular.

expectations.text: Cites evidence that benching grows lateral triceps more than long head
expectations.evidence: Does not cite any study showing lateral vs long head growth differences from benching.

expectations.text: References data (e.g., Duffy's dissertation) showing pecs/delts/lateral triceps increase activation similarly
expectations.evidence: Does not reference Duffy's dissertation or any specific study data.

expectations.text: Practical takeaway: don't abandon pec work; train bench-relevant musculature broadly
expectations.evidence: States: 'prioritizing triceps accessories over pec-focused work is a strategic mistake.'
